beats.text: Here's matrix A — entries two, one, eight, seven.
The pivot sits at row one, column one — that's two. A highlight marks it. The multiplier: eight divided by two equals four.

Build the elementary matrix E-two-one — identity with negative four in the lower-left. Entries: one, zero, negative four, one. Multiply: row two becomes row two minus four times row one. Eight, seven becomes zero, three.
Upper triangular matrix U appears: two, one, zero, three. Both pivots highlighted — two and three on the diagonal.
Now flip the sign on that multiplier. L is E-two-one inverted — entries one, zero, four, one. The negative four becomes positive four. That's it.
Check: L times U — one, zero, four, one times two, one, zero, three — multiplies back to two, one, eight, seven. Matrix A, recovered exactly. That's the L-U factorization.
